title: Lepisorus schraderi
taxonomy: Lepisorus schraderi is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is native to parts of Asia and belongs to the genus Lepisorus. Lepisorus schraderi is a type of lycopodiophytes, which is a group of vascular plants that belong to the division Pteridophyta. Lepisorus schraderi's classification also includes it being a member of the class Polypodiopsida and the phylum Tracheophyta. Further classification of this species places it within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Lepisorus schraderi, commonly known as Schrader's Lip Fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.
